aeolus-view-camp.title: Green Mountain News
aeolus-view-camp.quote: A new shelter was built on the Bondville Road. east of Manchester.

aeolus-view-camp.quote: The roof of the cabin on the Bondville road was blown away and a new one put on.

aeolus-view-camp.title: Trails and Summits of the Green Mountains
aeolus-view-camp.quote: It commands a view of Mount Aeolus near Manchester.

aeolus-view-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
aeolus-view-camp.quote: From this camp one gets a charming view of Mt Aeolus framed in trees across the Battenkill Valley.

aeolus-view-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
aeolus-view-camp.quote: There is no mention of this camp. only the view of Mt. Aeolus framed in trees across the Battenkill Valley.

aldrich-job-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
aldrich-job-camp.quote: One remaining abandoned building west of trail and north of road affords some shelter.

atlas-valley-shelter.author: George Pearlstein
atlas-valley-shelter.quote: Mr. Drown emphasized the need for recreational use of its extensive northern Vermont timberlands as an important aspect of their multiple use management.

badger-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
badger-camp.quote: There is no equipment at this camp.

badger-camp.title: Long Trail News
badger-camp.quote: This camp, old house, and barn are in poor shape but afford some shelter - not a GMC Camp.

barnes-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
barnes-camp.quote: Referred to as Barnes Camp Inn.

barnes-camp.title: Long Trail News
barnes-camp.quote: Barnes Camp in Smuggler's Notch will be open the coming season under the management of Mrs. H.

barrows-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
barrows-camp.quote: Good views of the Sterling Range.

barrows-camp.title: Long Trail News
barrows-camp.quote: It was called Barrows Camp as it was located on the Barrows Farm.

battell-lodge-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
battell-lodge-2.quote: Old wagon road goes out to Lincoln Valley via farms of the Atkins Brothers.

battell-lodge-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
battell-lodge-2.quote: Good water about 1/4 mile south near the Trail.

battell-lodge-2.title: Long Trail News
battell-lodge-2.quote: It is expected that this shelter will be speedily rebuilt as it is much in demand for first night lodging by those entering the Trail from the Pass.

beane-farm.title: Long Trail News
beane-farm.quote: She will often look. down upon her beloved Camel's Hump under whose shadows she spent so many happy years.

bear-hollow-shelter.title: Long Trail Guide Book
bear-hollow-shelter.quote: The water supply is a brook just south of the shelter along the LT.

bear-hollow-shelter.quote: The trail names have been changed to protect the guilty.

bear-hollow-shelter.quote: In attendance were Fred Gilbert, Jeff Harvey, George Bramhall, Smith Edwards, Tony Smith, Elizabeth Bramhall, Cindy Bigelow, Bob Lindemann, Paul and Joanne Woodward.

beaver-meadow-cabin.title: Long Trail Guide Book
beaver-meadow-cabin.quote: South of Molly Stark, turn south from Balcony, gradually descend to big tree with many arrows beside roofless Beaver Meadow Cabin.

beaver-meadow-cabin.title: Long Trail Guide Book
beaver-meadow-cabin.quote: Half-ruined log cabin gives some shelter.

beaver-meadow-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: The Sterling Section built in 1947 a fine log camp, called Beaver "Medder" Camp about 1 1/4 miles directly south, and 900 ft in elevation below Whiteface Shelter.

beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: Plant apple tree in the opening near the lodge for future springtime beauty and harvest time enjoyment.

beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: The Martins spotted what appeared to be a large hawk perched in a tree at the very top edge of the Notch cliff.

beaver-meadow-lodge.author: Bob Hagerman
beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: The bulk of this was carried out by a group of students at Sterling School, Craftsbury, and their leader Ron Rosen on a special trip May 22.

beaver-meadow-lodge.author: Bob Hagerman
beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: It immediately blossomed and bore fruit, 2 McIntosh apples and one orange!

beaver-meadow-lodge.author: Bob Hagerman
beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: No foreclosure is pending as the section is paid up on its rent!

beaver-meadow-lodge.author: Bob Hagerman
beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: Teillon and Steven patched up the door of the outhouse.

beaver-meadow-lodge.author: Bob Hagerman
beaver-meadow-lodge.quote: Work Trip June 14, 1975: Installed new stove pipe which had been carried in, patched two holes in the floor; made other minor repairs, carried out three bags of refuse ... apple tree looking quite healthy, though no apples, or even blossoms yet.

belvidere-mountain-foresters-cabin.title: Long Trail Guide Book

belvidere-mountain-foresters-cabin.quote: It is on the proposed trail relocation scheduled for completion in the spring of 1964.

belvidere-mountain-foresters-cabin.quote: The outhouse is-up the hill behind the shelter.

belvidere-mountain-foresters-cabin.title: Long Trail Guide Book
belvidere-mountain-foresters-cabin.quote: Water is from Bigelow Spring, 150 ft east of the shelter on the trail.

belvidere-mountain-foresters-cabin.title: Long Trail News
belvidere-mountain-foresters-cabin.quote: It was started in August 1961, and finished the first part of October 1961.

birch-glen-camp.title: Long Trail News
birch-glen-camp.quote: It will be close to an adequate water supply .and within easy walking distance of Hanksville, with its shopping and mail facilities.

birch-glen-camp.title: Long Trail News
birch-glen-camp.quote: The roof was also reversed to avoid the eaves to ridge pole drainage.

birch-glen-camp.title: Long Trail News
birch-glen-camp.quote: Don's foot overhang is now less than a foot

birch-glen-camp.title: Long Trail News
birch-glen-camp.quote: This year's work combined with the roofing done a few years ago by Tim Tierney should give Birch Glen another 70 years on the LT.

birch-glen-lodge.author: Walter O'Kane
birch-glen-lodge.title: Trails and Summits of the Green Mountains
birch-glen-lodge.quote: The location is well described by the name given to the lodge, Birch Glen.

birch-glen-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
birch-glen-lodge.quote: Permanent stream, good water 50 ft south of lodge; one place to dip water for oinking& cooking; place for bathing further downstream.

birch-glen-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
birch-glen-lodge.quote: By 1922 this lodge is an open camp with double-deck bunks for 12, good stove and simple cooking utensils.

blue-triangle-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
blue-triangle-camp.quote: Located in the woods on the bank of the crook.

blue-triangle-camp.title: Long Trail News
blue-triangle-camp.quote: It is intended more for use of the girls when on the Trail.

blue-triangle-camp.title: Long Trail News
blue-triangle-camp.quote: The only cabin reported seriously damaged by the Sept 21, 1938, storm.

blue-triangle-camp.title: Long Trail News
blue-triangle-camp.quote: The abandoned Shelter will be disassembled and moved to Spring Lake this summer.

bolton-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
bolton-lodge.quote: It has a good stove and bunks for 12 or more hikers.

bolton-lodge.title: Long Trail News
bolton-lodge.quote: The name has not yet been decided.

bolton-lodge.title: Long Trail News
bolton-lodge.quote: Along the area of frost-encrusted, fir-covered slopes, the sun cast a ribbon-like streak of gold, and where the slopes were untouched by the ribbon, a sheen of pink and purple was reflected - the perfect benediction to a grand party and for a new year.

bolton-lodge.author: Roy Buchanan
bolton-lodge.quote: They rebuilt about 3 feet of brick chimney, replaced several roof boards, shingled the whole front half of the roof, replaced chimney flashing, re-shingled near the chimney, put in two completely new windows, put in new boards for a whole bunk and 1/2 of another, replaced two settee backs, built up a toilet foundation, replaced the inside of a toilet, and cleaned up the area.

bolton-lodge.title: Long Trail News
bolton-lodge.quote: About half of the windowpanes have been broken this spring.

bourn-pond-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
bourn-pond-camp.quote: There are boats on Bourne Pond but none on Stratton.

bourn-pond-shelter-south.title: Long Trail Guide Book
bourn-pond-shelter-south.quote: Water is at a spring 250 ft east of the shelter.

boyce-shelter-3.title: Long Trail Guide Book
boyce-shelter-3.quote: Water, not always reliable, is from a small brook 200 ft north via the LT. It has been used from 1963 to present.

buchanan-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
buchanan-lodge.quote: Buchanan, founder of the LT Patrol in 1931-and for 36 years its active leader.

buchanan-shelter.title: Long Trail Guide Book
buchanan-shelter.quote: It has been in use from 1984 to the present.

buchanan-shelter.title: Long Trail News
buchanan-shelter.quote: Don Wallace and Jack Lance of the Burlington Section were leaders of the work party.

butler-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
butler-lodge.quote: It has been in use from 1933 to present.

butler-lodge.title: Long Trail News
butler-lodge.quote: Traveling on snowshoes, they used a small plastic sled to transport the stove to Butler where it was installed and, we trust, properly inaugurated with a good fire.

butler-lodge.title: Long Trail News
butler-lodge.quote: Butler was roofed four years ago so reroofing is not a concern.

camels-hump-clearing-huts.author: Walter O'Kane

camels-hump-clearing-huts.quote: The records show that 700 persons climbed the Hump in 1910 and many of them stayed overnight.

camels-hump-clearing-huts.title: Forest & Crag
camels-hump-clearing-huts.quote: It, in no sense, offers the ease and luxuries of the great houses of other mountain resorts, conceded the Camel's Hump Club.

camels-hump-clearing-huts.title: Long Trail Guide Book
camels-hump-clearing-huts.quote: Lodging 60 cents per night which includes one blanket, extra ones 10 cents each.

camels-hump-clearing-huts.title: Long Trail Guide Book
camels-hump-clearing-huts.quote: Two metal huts are mentioned and no caretaker.

camp-4.author: Lewis J. Paris
camp-4.title: The Making of the Long Trail
camp-4.quote: Two miles down the trail an abandoned lumber camp known as Camp No. 4 offers shelter, and two miles below this are found the mill and buildings of a lumber company now closed.

camp-4.author: Paul Woodward
camp-4.quote: Whether they were the same place or not, Camp #4 on the south slope of Bolton Mountain was the first GMC shelter which wasn't a care-taken summit camp that catered to the public.

camp-clarke.title: Long Trail News
camp-clarke.quote: Frappier for the Trustees reported that quite a bit of work had been done at the camp: cleaning up around the grounds, drying out the cellar, cleaning out the bar, installing a new pump at the well, etc. We regretfully leave Camp Cobb not later than May 1 next, at which time it is hoped that enough interest will have been aroused to build an addition to the camp in which to hold our activities in comfort.

camp-clarke.title: Long Trail News
camp-clarke.quote: A tree for every year with the president’s name inscribed upon it

camp-eleven.title: Long Trail Guide Book
camp-eleven.quote: This is one small shack left here from an old lumber camp, but at the present time not habitable except for hedgehogs.

camp-lizzie.title: Long Trail Guide Book
camp-lizzie.quote: Has beds and bedding, stove and utensils but is not a GMC camp.

carmel-camp-2.quote: The distance from New Boston to the camp is about a mile and a half and the rise in altitude is about 650 feet.

carmel-camp-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
carmel-camp-2.quote: Built in 1921 on the ruins of the former camp.

carmel-camp-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
carmel-camp-2.quote: This camp is now (spring 1947) in a collapsed unusable condition but will be rebuilt by the club as soon as materials and labor can be obtained.

carmel-camp-2.title: Long Trail News
carmel-camp-2.quote: Three camps; Carmel, Cooley Glen, and Hazen's Notch are down; a number of others are so old and dilapidated that they will require extensive repairs or replacement soon.

carmel-camp-3.title: Long Trail Guide Book
carmel-camp-3.quote: It was in use from 1949 to 1976.

carmel-camp-3.title: Long Trail Guide Book
carmel-camp-3.quote: It was enlarged in 1964with bunks for 7.

carmel-camp-3.author: Paul Woodward
carmel-camp-3.quote: That night they were on the roof doing their thing until we threw a 2-inch firecracker up to their party, after which they went back to the dump.

caughnawaga-shelter.title: Long Trail News
caughnawaga-shelter.quote: It should again be serviceable for several more years.

cloudland-shelter.quote: It has been in use from 1995 to present.

cooley-glen-shelter-2.quote: No, they can't throw their quills.

cooley-glen-shelter-3.quote: It has been in use from 1965 to present.

corliss-camp.quote: Thanks go to Woodbury's, Rice Lumber Co., Flanders Building Supply, Peter Salinger. and students from the Lamoille Vocational School; and Arjay West and Scout Troop 94 for their efforts.

duncan-camp.quote: The Duncan Camp of Boy Building has a camp in Peru, not far from the LT, and extends a cordial invitation to hikers over the trail to visit the camp and enjoy its hospitality.

dunsmoor-lodge.quote: Located 1.2 miles from the Bolton summit.

dunsmoor-lodge.quote: This is a club shelter with bunks for 12 persons.

dunsmoor-lodge.quote: Passing an abandoned lumber camp en route, they took over one of its buildings as a shelter, calling it Dunsmoor Lodge - the first of what was to become a system of - shelters along the entire length of the trail.

dunsmoor-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book (Supplement)
dunsmoor-lodge.quote: At some later date a new lodge will be located nearer Bolton.

dunsmoor-lodge.quote: His advice was heeded in due course and the present Bolton Lodge was built in 1928.

ellen-cabin.quote: This ruined cabin below the summit of Ellen was built in 1903.

emily-proctor-lodge-2.quote: The path then zigzags down steeply, makes a long circuit to the north and then to the southeast into the valley of the New Haven River, where it reaches Emily Proctor Lodge, a Club shelter beautifully situated in a deep glen near the foot of a rocky cascade.

emily-proctor-lodge-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
emily-proctor-lodge-2.quote: It is located at a waterfall in a deep gorge on the headwaters of the New Haven River.

fay-fuller-camp.quote: It was in use from. 1930 to 1972 or as long as required for trail relocation and shelter construction.

fay-fuller-camp.quote: Dr. Ross states that steps would be taken to protect it from damage hereafter, and to keep away depredators, with the aid of a man living nearby.

fay-fuller-camp.title: Long Trail News
fay-fuller-camp.quote: Other improvements include additional windows. bunks for ten, concrete floor, and a new stove.

fay-fuller-camp.title: Long Trail News
fay-fuller-camp.quote: The gate is barred to prevent parking in the field.

french-camp.quote: Mr. French also generously furnished lumber for the camp.

french-camp.quote: It was in use from 1931 to 1992 at which time it was destroyed.

french-camp.quote: Only work here was propping up the slightly tilted outhouse which the Yadanza apparently built.

french-camp.quote: French Camp is now in good condition, outwardly at least.

french-camp.author: Bob Hagerman
french-camp.quote: Work Trip July 12, 1975: Fred Judd & Lee Haselton applied a coat of roof tar to the roof and installed some new stovepipe.

gates-camp.quote: Two miles from the summit are the ruins of Gates Camp.

glen-ellen-lodge.quote: It has been in use from 1933 to present:

glen-ellen-lodge.quote: The camp is in good condition, and it should be good for another 32 years.

glen-ellen-shelter.quote: It was located 0.2 mile up the Barton Trail from the present-day lodge.

glen-ellen-shelter.quote: In front of the shelter is a little rock walled garden of native plants.

gorham-lodge.quote: This took time sweat and muscle.

gorham-lodge.quote: It has been in use from 1950 to present

gorham-lodge.quote: The mountains to the north were silhouetted, giving me a mystic feeling of grandeur.

greenwall-shelter-1.quote: Spring water intermittent 600 ft to southwest.

greenwall-shelter-2.quote: It has been in use from 1962 to present.

griffith-camp-2.quote: The Killington section has built an open camp in Mt Tabor with a rustic fireplace in front, at what is called the "old job", where the village of Griffith once stood.

griffith-camp-2.quote: The Club shelter was where the lumber road crossed Roaring Branch.

henry-davis-farm.quote: Lodging and meals may be obtained

henry-davis-farm.quote: Good meals and lodging President's Section at Elev. 1520 ft

henry-davis-farm.title: Long Trail News
henry-davis-farm.quote: Davis's daughter We hear it well spoken of.

henry-davis-farm.title: Long Trail News
henry-davis-farm.quote: Announcement is made of the death of Mrs. Henry Davis, at the age of 83 She and her husband were famous for the entertainment of thousands of long Trailers at thee home in Lincoln, over a space of many years Many members who have enjoyed their hospitality will remember them

honted-house-camp.author: Walter O'Kane

honted-house-camp.quote: It was not a Club shelter, but use was granted to the Club.

honted-house-camp.quote: An old camp fitted up with bunks.

inn-at-long-trail.quote: Parcels are accepted and held, if marked “Hold for Long Trail Hiker.” Parcels are accepted from the U S Postal Service or UPS

jay-camp-1.title: Long Trail Guide Book
jay-camp-1.quote: This camp was built in 1936, a closed frame camp with inside fireplace, bunks for 8 to 12 and spring water.

jay-camp-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
jay-camp-2.quote: It has been in use from 1958 to present.

jay-camp-2.title: Long Trail News
jay-camp-2.quote: It steeps 8 to 12 persons and cost $515.33.

kid-gore-shelter.title: Long Trail News
kid-gore-shelter.quote: The best route may still be locked beneath several feet of snow and still in the hands of Mother Nature.

kid-gore-shelter.title: Long Trail News
kid-gore-shelter.quote: His most important contribution to the Long Trail was the part he played in shaping the lives of so many young men.

kid-gore-shelter.title: Long Trail News
kid-gore-shelter.quote: Due to its hilly and rocky location, it does not have a "Monticello" type front lawn.

kiln-camp.title: Long Trail Guide Book
kiln-camp.quote: Bunks in an old charcoal kiln.

lake-mansfield-trout-club.quote: Meals may be had, and overnight lodging is usually obtainable unless rooms are need by club members.

lake-pleiad-shelter-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
lake-pleiad-shelter-2.quote: It was mentioned in O'Kane's, 1926, as a Lodge built of boards and provided with a double tier of bunks at each end.

lake-pleiad-shelter-2.quote: The register at Lake Pleiad had 625 names in 1928.

lake-pleiad-shelter-2.title: Long Trail News

lake-pleiad-shelter-2.title: Long Trail News
lake-pleiad-shelter-2.quote: Thanks for this job goes to George Pearlstein and a group of his Scouts and Troop Committeemen.

lake-pleiad-shelter-2.quote: The dump needs to be cleaned and the water supply should be protected with a cover and a pipe, to be done at a later date.

lake-pleiad-shelter-2.title: Long Trail News
lake-pleiad-shelter-2.quote: The Breadloaf Section, especially Guy Wallis was responsible for its removal.

laura-woodward-shelter-1.quote: They spent three months almost entirely camped in the woods, not only cutting the trail but also building Laura Woodward Shelter.

laura-woodward-shelter-1.quote: This camp is built of logs, open front. stone fireplace, water, bunks for 6 or 8.

laura-woodward-shelter-2.quote: A lumber road gave access on the east which reduced cost to $386.14.

laura-woodward-shelter-2.title: Long Trail Guide Book
laura-woodward-shelter-2.quote: It has been in use from 1956 to present.

little-rock-pond-shelter-1.title: Long Trail Guide Book

little-rock-pond-shelter-2.quote: Jr. served as a GMC caretaker at this shelter in 1972.

little-rock-pond-shelter-2.author: Paul Woodward
little-rock-pond-shelter-2.quote: We slept soundly on the dirt floor without pads that night, as I recall.

long-trail-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
long-trail-lodge.quote: It was destroyed by fire in 1968.

long-trail-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
long-trail-lodge.quote: This Lodge had been sold.

long-trail-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
long-trail-lodge.quote: Proctor and his mother, Mrs. Fletcher D.

long-trail-lodge.title: Long Trail Guide Book
long-trail-lodge.quote: One of the very interesting features of the Long Trail Lodge is the identification and marking of the plant life, shrubs, and trees in the vicinity of the Lodge by Mr. Maurice Broun, the Club naturalist, who spends the season at the Lodge and is available to parties who wish to study the botany of the Green Mountains.

long-trail-lodge.title: Green Mountain News
long-trail-lodge.quote: Proctor of Proctor, who has been most generous in financial aid to the club, has started the subscription with $200, and another friend has subscribed $100.

long-trail-lodge.title: Green Mountain News
long-trail-lodge.quote: Proctor would donate the furnishings of the house and supply the water.

long-trail-lodge.title: Green Mountain News
long-trail-lodge.quote: A buffet lunch was served.

long-trail-lodge.title: Green Mountain News
long-trail-lodge.quote: The price for members will be $1 per night.

long-trail-lodge.title: Green Mountain News
long-trail-lodge.quote: The road from the east to the clubhouse will be rebuilt this coming summer, and while it is said a passable way will be kept open during the work, we recommend that during operations the house be approached from the west, via Rutland.

long-trail-lodge.title: Long Trail News

He also suggested that the board of trustees ought to take more responsibility in the operation of the Lodge.
